member.name: Joani Reid
member.role: MP for East Kilbride and Strathaven

addresses: Unit 9, Jacobean House, 1A Glebe Street, East Kilbride, G74 4LY

member.synopsis: Joani Reid is the Independent MP for East Kilbride and Strathaven , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what the average waiting time was between approval of an Access to Work application and support being put in place in 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to reduce waiting times for Access to Work (a) assessments and (b) decisions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Maintenance Service: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to help improve (a) response times to (i) queries and (ii) complaints and (b) other communication between the Child Maintenance Service and service users.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the effectiveness of the Access to Work scheme in supporting disabled people to (a) enter and (b) remain in employment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Torness Power Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, if he has made an estimate of the total output of Torness nuclear power station over the last year and what proportion of Scottish generation this represents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to my hon. Friend in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether his Department made an assessment of the impact of zonal pricing of electricity on inward investment into Scottish low carbon electricity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to my hon. Friend in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what representations he has received from the Scottish Government on the issue of zonal pricing for electricity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to my hon. Friend in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many days in the last year the electricity grid in Scotland was supplied with electricity generated elsewhere in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to my hon. Friend in the time available before Prorogation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: East Kilbride
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent engagement she has had with trade unions representing FCDO staff in East Kilbride on restructuring proposals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) is committed to its three locations in the UK: King Charles Street in London, Hanslope Park in Milton Keynes and Abercrombie House in East Kilbride. Location is not a factor when making decisions in ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: East Kilbride
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of proposed restructuring within the FCDO on staff based in East Kilbride.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) is committed to its three locations in the UK: King Charles Street in London, Hanslope Park in Milton Keynes and Abercrombie House in East Kilbride. Location is not a factor when making decisions in ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coronavirus: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make a statement on the availability of covid booster vaccinations to lung cancer patients receiving a targeted therapy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to protecting those most vulnerable to COVID-19 through vaccination, as guided by the independent Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI). The primary aim of the national COVID-19 vaccination programme remains th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether he has an estimate for the share of electricity in demand in Scotland met by nuclear generation for each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Electricity, including that generated from nuclear, is traded both nationally and internationally and is not constrained to geographical areas. No estimate has been made of the contribution of nuclear generation to Scotland’s demand. Data on Scotland’s an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the effectiveness of the Child Maintenance Service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Child Maintenance Service (CMS) is committed to providing a modern and efficient service for all customers. CMS continues to exceed key performance indicators, including application clearances, change of circumstances clearances, Collect and Pay compl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps he is taking to reform the Child Maintenance Service system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced reforms to strengthen the Child Maintenance Service (CMS), including its intention to remove Direct Pay and move to a single, enhanced Collect and Pay system. This will enable the CMS to monitor all payments, identify noncompl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Sexual Abuse Material: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the use of AI by child sexual abuse offenders on levels of offending.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the serious and evolving threat posed by artificial intelligence being misused by offenders for child sexual abuse.AI-generated child sexual abuse material is not a victimless crime; it often depicts real children, increasing the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to support defence sector jobs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Defence Industrial Strategy, we launched a £182 million skills package that encompasses a range of initiatives which will provide people with the necessary skills to work in the defence sector. Proving that Defence is an engine for growth, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Concrete
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when her Department plans to publish its findings on weight limits for Volumetric Concrete Mixers; and whether the review considers the need (a) for competition in the market and (b) to reduce emissions across
writtenQuestions.answerText: The outcome of my Department’s review into volumetric concrete mixers was published on 18 March. This can be found at the following link:https://www.gov.uk/government/calls-for-evidence/volumetric-concrete-mixers-review The implications for road safety, i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: East Kilbride
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the proposed relocation of his Department's site at East Kilbride on the local community.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The FCDO is awaiting a decision from HM Treasury on the Business Case submitted for the FCDO moving its joint HQ in Scotland from East Kilbride to Glasgow. The FCDO has not made any specific assessment of the impact to the local community of a decision to...

writtenQuestions.heading: Manufacturing Industries: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Scotland, what recent discussions he has had with the Secretary of State for Business and Trade on the future of industry in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have regular discussions with the Secretary of State for Business and Trade to ensure we work together to safeguard the future for key Scottish industries.We inherited a fiscal crisis, and in Scotland an industrial crisis as well. Our industrial strateg...
